name: Adaptive RAG
description: Route the query before retrieving: answer simple questions directly, search the local knowledge base for grounded ones, or fall back to the web — so not every request pays the retrieval cost.

data.label: How it works
data.text: Adaptive RAG decides up front HOW to answer. A Router classifies the question into 'vectorstore', 'websearch', or 'direct', so only questions that need grounding trigger retrieval. Contrast with Corrective RAG, which grades AFTER retrieving.

data.label: Route Query
data.routingPrompt: Decide how to answer: 'vectorstore' for questions about our documented knowledge, 'websearch' for current/external facts, 'direct' for general questions needing no retrieval.

data.label: Web Search
data.description: Search the web for current or external information.

data.label: Generator
data.systemPrompt: Answer the question. If retrieved context is present, ground your answer in it; otherwise answer directly.

data.label: Try it
data.text: Simulate with an input containing the word 'vectorstore', 'websearch', or 'direct' to take that path — the Router matches a route name in the text.
